A shrimp's heart is located in its head! 🦐❤️
Shrimp can swim backwards by rapidly flexing their tail and abdomen. 🏊‍♂️
There are over 2,000 different species of shrimp worldwide! 🌍
Mantis shrimp can punch with the force of a .22 caliber bullet! 💥
Some shrimp species can live up to 6-7 years. 🎂
The pistol shrimp can create a sound louder than a gunshot by snapping its claw! 🔫
Shrimp come in a rainbow of colors including pink, brown, white, red, and even blue! 🌈
Cleaner shrimp help remove parasites from fish, making them nature's dental hygienists! 🦷
Some shrimp can change their gender during their lifetime. 🔄
Shrimp blood is blue due to copper-based hemocyanin instead of iron-based hemoglobin. 💙
Baby shrimp are called 'larvae' and look nothing like adult shrimp! 👶
Shrimp have excellent vision with compound eyes on movable stalks. 👀
The brine shrimp can survive in waters ten times saltier than the ocean! 🧂
Shrimp communicate through chemical signals and antenna touching. 📡
Some deep-sea shrimp are bioluminescent and can produce their own light! ✨
Shrimp molt their exoskeleton as they grow, sometimes eating it for nutrients! 🍽️
The largest shrimp species can grow up to 16 inches (40 cm) long! 📏
Shrimp have 10 legs (decapods), but they also have swimming legs called pleopods! 🦵
A group of shrimp is called a 'colony' or 'troupe.' 👥
Shrimp taste with their feet! They have chemoreceptors on their legs. 👣
Freshwater shrimp can live in temperatures ranging from near freezing to tropical waters. 🌡️
Some shrimp form symbiotic relationships with sea anemones for protection. 🤝
Fairy shrimp swim upside down! They use their 11 pairs of legs to propel themselves. 🙃
Shrimp are an important food source for many marine animals, from small fish to whales. 🐟
The mantis shrimp has the most complex eyes in the animal kingdom with 16 color receptors! 🌈👁️
Snapping shrimp colonies can produce enough noise to interfere with submarine sonar! 🚢
Shrimp have been sent to space! They were studied aboard the Space Shuttle. 🚀
Some shrimp species are transparent, making them nearly invisible in water! 👻
Certain shrimp can regenerate lost limbs after molting. 🔄
The global shrimp industry is worth billions of dollars annually! 💰
